mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate lower the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing present values of future corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 6.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer spending, which reduces corporate revenues and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 6.0, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand growth, which boosts corporate profit margins and earnings growth, generating positive equity market returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when both series grow, or the interaction term yields a negative coefficient in multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid month-over-month increases in the industrial production index reflect accelerating factory output and capacity utilization, which precedes earnings revisions and drives positive short-term equity returns during recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns during recovery periods, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds 255 on the CPI index while the federal funds rate remains near zero, real rates turn deeply negative, triggering expectations of aggressive central bank tightening that compresses equity valuations and causes negative returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally by more than 2% over 20 days when CPIAUCSL exceeds 255 and FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5%, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 500,000 signal an overheating labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, leading to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative or flat in over 60% of months where payroll growth exceeds 500k, or the threshold signal shows no incremental predictive value over baseline trends.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate strong consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which spills over into broader economic growth and generates positive short-term equity market momentum.
failure_condition: HOUST increases consistently precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns, or the series exhibits a Rank IC below 0.01 across all tested market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining industrial production and rising unemployment confirms a broad-based economic contraction, which destroys corporate cash flows and forces sustained negative equity returns during recessionary periods.
failure_condition: The joint signal predicts positive returns during confirmed recessions, or the interaction between falling INDPRO and rising UNRATE fails to improve classification accuracy over a 10-year out-of-sample test.
